content:
**Pacers Shock NBA, Claim Eastern Conference Title for First Time Since 2000**

Indianapolis, IN — June 5, 2025

In a stunning playoff campaign, the Indiana Pacers have clinched the 2025 NBA Eastern Conference Championship, defeating the heavily favored Boston Celtics in a thrilling Game 6 at Gainbridge Fieldhouse. This victory marks the franchise’s first Eastern Conference title in 25 years and propels them to the NBA Finals.

Led by All-Star guard Tyrese Haliburton and breakout forward Bennedict Mathurin, the Pacers rode a wave of resilience and teamwork throughout the postseason. “We believed in each other from day one,” Haliburton said during the trophy presentation. “No one outside this locker room expected this, but we knew what we were capable of.”

Indiana’s path to the Finals was anything but easy. After dispatching the New York Knicks in the Conference Semifinals, the Pacers overcame the Celtics’ star-studded roster with suffocating defense and timely shooting from veterans Myles Turner and Buddy Hield. Head Coach Rick Carlisle praised his players’ grit: “This group never backs down. They earned every bit of this.”

The victory electrified Pacers fans, who flooded downtown Indianapolis in celebration. The city now looks ahead with hope as the team prepares for its first Finals appearance since the days of Reggie Miller. “This is for every Pacers fan who’s waited so long,” said Mathurin, grinning. “We’re not done yet.”
